subject: Public Health
title: Food Fortification Programs
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Food Fortification Programs.

hook.text: Adding nutrients to everyday foods has quietly prevented more disease than most medicines.

buildup.text: Iodized salt, folic acid in flour, and vitamin D in milk are all examples of food fortification.

discovery.text: Fortifying flour with folic acid reduced neural tube birth defects by 30-70% wherever implemented.

twist.text: Over 140 countries mandate some form of food fortification—yet billions still miss out.

climax.text: Fortification costs just pennies per person per year and reaches entire populations silently.

punchline.text: The cheapest health intervention hides inside staple foods.

quiz.question: What did adding folic acid to flour prevent?
options: Neural tube birth defects reduced by 30-70%
